The Breakfast Club · Special edition

Is your brand
AI‑ready?

Follow the questions below to find out where your organisation sits across the four pillars of AI search maturity. Pick the answer that honestly reflects where you are now, not where you hope to be.

Up to 5 questions 4 maturity levels Based on the AI search maturity framework
Start here
When AI search comes up in a meeting, what typically happens?
Q1
Pillar 1 · Technical SEO
Do you know how AI crawlers interact with your site, or is that visibility gap still open?
Q2
Pillar 2 · Earned authority
Is AI search considered when planning your SEO, Digital PR or content activity?
Q3
Pillar 2 · Earned authority
How intentional is your approach to building earned authority and credibility online?
Q4
Pillar 3 · Content & contextualisation
Is your content built around real audience questions and use cases, or primarily around keywords?
Q5
Pillar 4 · Measurement
Are you tracking AI search visibility, citation patterns or share of search in any structured way?
Q6
Pillar 1 · Technical SEO
When a new AI search trend or platform update appears, how does your team typically respond?
Q7
Pillar 2 · Earned authority
How well do your SEO, content, Digital PR and brand teams share audience insight and work toward shared goals?
Q8
Pillar 3 · Content & contextualisation
When AI search results shift, how does your content and visibility strategy respond?
Q9
Pillar 4 · Measurement & data
Does your measurement framework account for AI search attribution, including zero-click and upper-funnel impact?
Your result
🔍
Reactive
Not yet set up for AI search

AI systems don't yet have strong, consistent signals about your brand's authority, relevance or expertise. Issues only surface when someone flags them, and there's no structured approach to monitoring or improving AI visibility. Most organisations sit here right now. Early movers still have real ground to gain.

Where to start Secure access to server logs and check how AI crawlers interact with your site. Begin mapping where you appear — and where competitors do — across ChatGPT, Perplexity and AI Overviews.
The Breakfast Club  ·  Brave Bison  ·  2026
Your result
🌱
Adaptive
Experimenting, but not yet consistent

Your teams understand that AI search matters and are starting to explore what it means in practice. Known issues are entering backlogs, some server logs are being reviewed, and Digital PR is beginning to build authority. The gap between knowing the problem and fixing it is narrowing, but execution still varies across pillars.

Next move Focus on cross-team alignment. Digital PR, content and technical SEO working from shared audience insight, rather than in silos, will accelerate your progress significantly.
The Breakfast Club  ·  Brave Bison  ·  2026
Your result
🧠
Strategic
AI search is embedded in your planning

Your teams treat AI search as a genuine acquisition channel. Technical readiness, earned authority, content quality and measurement are coordinated rather than siloed. AI crawler data informs sprint priorities, content reflects full audience journeys, and Digital PR activity is sustained and intentional.

Next move Shift from reactive optimisation to predictive positioning. Build systems to detect early signals in crawler behaviour, platform updates and citation shifts, and act before competitors notice.
The Breakfast Club  ·  Brave Bison  ·  2026
Your result
🔮
Predictive
You anticipate change before competitors do

Your organisation designs for where AI search is heading, not just where it is today. You're tracking early signals, running structured tests, building authority ahead of trends, and positioning your brand as the entity AI systems trust by default. AI search signals feed directly into forecasting and attribution models.

Keep going Explore agentic commerce readiness. AI systems completing tasks or purchases on behalf of users is projected to reach $1 trillion by 2030. Make sure your technical and content foundations are ready for that shift.
